blocks.text: The variable valve timing mechanism [VTC (Variable valve Timing Control)] installed in this vehicle continuously changes the opening and closing timing of intake valves according to engine speed, vehicle speed, and engine load, thereby improving fuel economy, reducing emissions, and increasing output. This is achieved by continuously changing the opening and closing timing of intake valves according to engine speed, vehicle speed, and engine load.
In the event of a VTEC failure, the fail-safe function stops VTC control and fixes it at the slowest angle position, preventing engine damage and guaranteeing operational performance.
The VTC actuator consists of a hydraulically operated vane and timing gear integrated housing that is mounted on the intake camshaft.

The VTC hydraulic valve is activated by a signal from the PGM-FI ECU and applies hydraulic pressure to the advance and delay angle hydraulic chambers of the VTC actuator to rotate the vane section, thereby continuously changing the intake camshaft phase. The VTC hydraulic valve is fitted with a strainer to prevent foreign objects from being entrapped.
The PGM-FI ECU calculates and determines the control amount based on signals from each sensor and performs feedback control of the VTC hydraulic valve.

The spool valve of the VTC hydraulic valve moves, hydraulic pressure is applied to the advance angle hydraulic chamber of the VTC actuator, and the intake camshaft rotates in the advance angle direction.
The spool valve of the VTC hydraulic valve moves, hydraulic pressure is applied to the retardation hydraulic chamber of the VTC actuator, and the intake camshaft rotates in the retardation direction.
